prompt: Customs has flagged one of your artists' visas at the airport. The show is in six hours.

outcomes.description: The lawyers sort it out. Everyone makes the show with time to spare.
Legal can't resolve it in time. The artist misses the show entirely.
The backup steps up brilliantly. The audience never knows.
Local fans specifically came for the detained artist. Disappointment spreads.

answers.label: Cancel and issue a public apology

outcomes.description: Professional and transparent. It costs you tonight but protects the tour's reputation.

prompt: You've been told a major international label A&R scout is in the audience tonight.

answers.label: Tailor the set to showcase commercial appeal

outcomes.description: The scout is impressed. A meeting is requested for tomorrow morning.
The crowd can feel the set is being performed for someone. The energy dips.

answers.label: Play your most authentic fan-favourite set

outcomes.description: The scout loves the authenticity. They want to sign without compromising the sound.
The scout wanted something more radio-friendly. They leave after four songs.

answers.label: Don't tell your artists the scout is there

outcomes.description: Natural performance wins the scout over completely. No nerves, just talent.
Your artist finds out and is furious. Pre-show blow-up.

prompt: Another act drops out of a festival. You're offered their headline slot — two hours from now.

answers.label: Take it — this is the opportunity of a lifetime

outcomes.description: Your artists rise to the occasion. A genuinely career-defining performance.
Underprepared for the scale. Visible stumbles in front of ten times your usual crowd.
A perfectly timed mid-bill set to a primed crowd. Solid win.
The festival gives the slot to someone else. You stay where you were.
